It is native to Western Australia and is a member of the genus Eriostemon, which consists of several species of shrubs and small trees. The Rutaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants that includes many well-known species such as oranges, lemons, and limes. intro: Eriostemon pleurandroides, also known as the 'Fiddle Bush', is a shrub native to Australia, belonging to the Rutaceae family of plants. The Fiddle Bush is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, providing relief from various health issues such as skin irritations and respiratory infections. The plant contains a range of bioactive constituents including sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and coumarins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Eriostemon pleurandroides is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, with its most common preparations being used to soothe skin conditions and reduce inflammation.
